author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Pelagius Avoids the Question as to Why Baptism is Necessary for Infants.
title: Pelagius Avoids the Question as to Why Baptism is Necessary for Infants.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 21 [XIX.]—Pelagius Avoids the Question as to Why Baptism is Necessary for Infants.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now it is to no purpose that he says all this.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He does not clear himself thereby.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Not even they have ever denied the impossibility of infants entering the kingdom of heaven without baptism.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But this is not the question; what we are discussing concerns the obliteration of original sin in infants.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Let him clear himself on this point, since he refuses to acknowledge that there is anything in infants which the laver of regeneration has to cleanse.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: On this account we ought carefully to consider what he has afterwards to say.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: After adducing, then, the passage of the Gospel which declares that “whosoever is not born again of water and the Spirit cannot enter into the kingdom of heaven” (on which matter, as we have said, they raise no question), he goes on at once to ask: “Who indeed is so impious as to have the heart to refuse the common redemption of the human race to an infant of any age whatever?”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But this is ambiguous language; for what redemption does he mean?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Is it from evil to good? or from good to better?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now even Cœlestius, at Carthage, allowed a redemption for infants in his book; although, at the same time, he would not admit the transmission of sin to them from Adam.
